medications.drug_name: paring of overlying keratin + aperture ('doughnut') offloading pad (clinician/podiatry)
medications.indication_rationale: Reduces the mass and transfers weight off the lesion to relieve the mechanical pain immediately.
Definitive treatment of the wart over weeks; cryotherapy/podiatry if OTC fails.
Pumicing spreads the virus to other sites — avoid.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the offloading pad in place, apply salicylic acid to the centre daily, don't pumice, and wear thongs in public showers.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Relieves pain, treats the wart, and prevents spread/reinfection.

patient_education_points.point: This is a plantar wart — a common skin-virus lump that acts like a stone in your shoe. Paring the thick skin and a doughnut pad take the pressure off and stop the pain.
Use the salicylic-acid paint on the centre daily for weeks (or have it frozen); don't pumice it (that spreads it), and wear thongs in public showers.
If the area becomes hot, red, swollen or oozes thick pus, or the pain becomes a constant throb even at rest — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to topical therapy; cryotherapy/podiatry if not resolving.

must_include_items: Diagnose a plantar wart (disrupted skin lines/black dots) vs corn/foreign body and exclude deep infection/neuropathic ulcer
Mechanical relief now (paring + aperture offloading pad)
Topical salicylic acid ± cryotherapy/podiatry; avoid pumicing; footwear/hygiene
errors_of_commission: Treating as a simple corn only
Over-triage to ED for abscess/foreign body without red flags
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires wart diagnosis + mechanical offloading/pain relief + definitive therapy, with a deep-infection safety-net.
